title: Maximizing Shop Revenue with Professional Diagnostic Tools
author: Mike Miller
excerpt: Investing in professional diagnostic equipment is not an expense — it is a revenue generator. The right tools allow your shop to take on more jobs, complete them faster, and charge premium rates....
content:
Investing in professional diagnostic equipment is not an expense — it is a revenue generator. The right tools allow your shop to take on more jobs, complete them faster, and charge premium rates.

Consider this: every vehicle you turn away because your scan tool does not support it is lost revenue. Every hour your technician spends struggling with a slow or limited diagnostic tool is lost productivity. Every job you send to the dealer because you cannot perform the required programming or calibration is money walking out your door.

Texa diagnostic tools are designed to maximize your shop's capabilities. With the broadest vehicle coverage, fastest hardware, and deepest system access available, Texa allows you to say yes to more jobs and complete them efficiently. The return on investment typically comes within months, not years.

title: Diesel Diagnostics: What Makes Texa Stand Out
author: Mike Miller
excerpt: Diesel vehicles present unique diagnostic challenges that many scan tools handle poorly. Texa has deep roots in diesel diagnostics, particularly for heavy trucks and commercial vehicles....
content:
Diesel vehicles present unique diagnostic challenges that many scan tools handle poorly. Texa has deep roots in diesel diagnostics, particularly for heavy trucks and commercial vehicles.

Diesel engines rely on high-pressure fuel injection systems, turbocharger systems, exhaust aftertreatment systems including DPF and SCR, and complex engine management systems. Diagnosing these systems requires specialized knowledge and tools that can access manufacturer-specific parameters and run active tests.

Texa's diagnostic platforms provide comprehensive diesel coverage including forced DPF regeneration, injector coding, turbo actuator testing, AdBlue system diagnostics, and detailed fuel system analysis. For shops that work on diesel trucks and commercial vehicles, Texa is widely considered the go-to diagnostic solution.

title: How Texa Supports Independent Shops Against Dealers
author: Mike Miller
excerpt: Independent repair shops have long faced a competitive disadvantage when it comes to diagnostic capabilities. Dealer tools have historically offered deeper access and more advanced functions. Texa is ...
content:
Independent repair shops have long faced a competitive disadvantage when it comes to diagnostic capabilities. Dealer tools have historically offered deeper access and more advanced functions. Texa is closing that gap.

Texa's comprehensive vehicle coverage and deep system access give independent shops the ability to perform many of the same diagnostic and programming functions that were previously only available at the dealer. This includes module coding, key programming, injector coding, DPF regeneration, and many other advanced procedures.

The Right to Repair movement has made significant progress in ensuring that independent shops have access to the same diagnostic information as dealers. Texa has been a strong supporter of this movement, and their diagnostic platforms reflect that commitment by providing the broadest possible access to vehicle systems across all makes and models.
